checks.finding: design.md contains 5 inline ADRs (ADR-001 through ADR-005) and a detailed Directory Structure section that together fully describe component boundaries (new sdd-review-loop plugin, modifications to sdd-bootstrap, sdd-bootstrap-interviewer, sdd-quality-loop, sdd-implementation), integration sequencing (5-phase migration order in ADR-002/ADR-003), and technology choices (bare relative path resolution in ADR-004, no marketplace.json registration in ADR-005). Coverage is sufficient for a refactoring spec.

No circular dependencies detected. Dependency flow is strictly one-directional: sdd-bootstrap → sdd-review-loop (caller to callee), sdd-bootstrap-interviewer → sdd-review-loop (caller to callee). sdd-quality-loop hosts the hook guard independently and is not called by sdd-review-loop. The new sdd-review-loop plugin has no outgoing calls to its callers.

No database schema changes are involved. design.md explicitly acknowledges report path impact under 'Migration & backward compatibility': report paths reports/impl-review/ and reports/task-review/ are confirmed unchanged after migration, so implReviewVerdictExists path resolution requires no update. The JSON schema for integrated-verdict.json is referenced in the Python Check 2e specification (verdict field values PASS / PASS-with-warnings). Data impact is adequately covered.

All call sites identified in INV-005 are addressed. design.md's Directory Structure section and doc plan name sdd-bootstrap/SKILL.md L88/L99 and sdd-bootstrap-interviewer/SKILL.md as the caller update targets (REQ-004). docs/skill-reference.md L786-788 and L851-853 are enumerated for invocation-example updates. The skill invocation interface (plugin-qualified names /sdd-review-loop:impl-review-loop and /sdd-review-loop:task-review-loop) is fully specified.

ADR-003 provides a detailed two-phase PROTECTED_GATE_SUFFIXES update strategy: 6 new paths added before new plugin creation (T-002 Phase 1), 6 old paths removed only after old plugin deletion (T-002 Phase 5), achieving a zero-gap protection window. The human/sudo session requirement is explicitly stated as a hard constraint. Python Check 2e (_impl_review_verdict_exists) is fully specified with implementation code, covering the security boundary gap identified in INV-002. PROTECTED_GATE_SUFFIXES as a security boundary is properly addressed.
SKIP — feature_type is cli/library (internal plugin system refactoring). No frontend/backend split exists.

acceptance-tests.md defines 10 ACs covering all 6 REQs. AC-006 explicitly mandates bash tests/guard-parity.tests.sh with Scenarios 19/20/21 added for Check 2e JS/Python parity (REQ-006). AC-008 provides regression coverage via scenario.tests.sh and install.tests.sh for BL-010/BL-011. AC-004/AC-005 cover the Python Check 2e positive/negative cases. The design.md Python Check 2e specification provides sufficient detail for test implementation.

Every component referenced in design.md is either newly defined within it or traceable to investigation.md: sdd-review-loop (new plugin, fully defined in Directory Structure), sdd-bootstrap / sdd-bootstrap-interviewer / sdd-quality-loop / sdd-implementation / sdd-ship / sdd-lite (all confirmed as established system components via investigation.md STR-001, INV-001, INV-005, INV-006). Scripts sdd-hook-guard.py, sdd-hook-guard.js, validate-repository.ps1, guard-parity.tests.sh are all evidenced in investigation.md findings (INV-002, INV-003, INV-005). No undefined component references found.

ADR-001 through ADR-005 are all present inline in design.md (lines 7-58), each with Decision, Rationale, and (where applicable) Rejected Alternatives sections. No separate docs/adr/ files are needed or referenced. Inline ADR embedding is acceptable for this refactoring scope.
